title: Add `assistant_turn_observed` + `cite_policy_activated` event schemas to event-ledger discriminated union
description: Register two new event variants in the Zod discriminated union eventLedgerEventSchema: (1) `assistant_turn_observed` — captures per-turn KB: line emission with kb_line_raw, cite_ids[], cite_tags[], client, turn_id, session_id; (2) `cite_policy_activated` — idempotent marker emitted on first --cite-coverage invocation, carries policy version and timestamp. Both spread the standard envelope (kind/id/ts/schema_version). Roundtrip tests assert write→read shape preservation.

files.target: schema definitions + discriminated union (L330-L443) + type union (L478-L511)
files.change: Add `assistantTurnObservedEventSchema` (envelope + event_type literal 'assistant_turn_observed' + kb_line_raw: z.string().nullable() + cite_ids: z.array(z.string()).default([]) + cite_tags: z.array(z.enum(['planned','recalled','chained-from','dismissed','none'])).default([]) + client: z.enum(['cc','codex','cursor']).optional() + turn_id: z.string() + envelope_index: z.number().int().nonnegative().optional() + timestamp: z.string().datetime()). Add `citePolicyActivatedEventSchema` (envelope + event_type literal 'cite_policy_activated' + policy_version: z.string() + timestamp: z.string().datetime()). Append both to eventLedgerEventSchema.discriminatedUnion array. Append type aliases AssistantTurnObservedEvent + CitePolicyActivatedEvent to EventLedgerEvent union.

Add two parallel roundtrip tests: (a) append + read assistant_turn_observed event preserves kb_line_raw/cite_ids/cite_tags/client/turn_id; (b) append + read cite_policy_activated event preserves policy_version/timestamp. Mirror existing doctorRunEventSchema roundtrip test pattern.

implementation: Read packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts L1-L60 (envelope shape) and L330-L443 (doctor precedent + union site) to confirm patterns.
Define assistantTurnObservedEventSchema as a const z.object spreading eventLedgerEnvelopeSchema.shape + the new fields; export the schema and TS type via z.infer.
Define citePolicyActivatedEventSchema with minimal fields (policy_version + timestamp + envelope) — mirrors doctorRunEventSchema shape.
Append both to the discriminated union (preserve comment-header style 'v2.0.0-rc.20 TASK-02').
Append type aliases to EventLedgerEvent union near L478-L511.
Add roundtrip tests in event-ledger.test.ts using appendEventLedgerEvent + readEventLedger flow.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared test schemas` + `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test event-ledger` to confirm green.

reference.pattern: Zod discriminated union extension (rc.7 T5 doctorRunEventSchema precedent)
reference.examples: doctorRunEventSchema is the structural twin: literal event_type, minimal fields, appended to union + type alias.

rationale.chosen_approach: Single discriminated-union registration for both new events in one commit — shared schema is a unit; partial registration breaks appendEventLedgerEvent for the unregistered variant.
alternatives_considered: Split into two separate commits (rejected: artificial — both events are part of the same policy contract and would force TASK-03 + TASK-04 to depend on different commits)
Use generic event_type:'cite' with a sub_kind discriminator (rejected: violates existing discriminated-union pattern, complicates readEventLedger filter)
decision_factors: Atomic schema additions match existing rc.X TASK-Y commit precedent
Both events part of same protocol surface
rationale.tradeoffs: Slightly larger single commit, but the bytes-of-change are small (two schema objects + union entries + type aliases + two tests).

manual_checks: Confirm appendEventLedgerEvent accepts a sample assistant_turn_observed payload without throwing
Confirm readEventLedger returns the parsed event with all fields intact
success_metrics: Both new schemas exported and reachable from @fenglimg/fabric-shared barrel
Roundtrip test passes: write event → read event → assert deep-equal on shape
0 typescript errors across shared + server packages

criteria: Both schemas present in eventLedgerEventSchema.discriminatedUnion union
Both TS type aliases present in EventLedgerEvent type union
Roundtrip tests pass: append → read preserves all fields including cite_tags enum + kb_line_raw + turn_id
Zero typescript errors in shared + server packages
Commit msg: `feat(rc20): event ledger variants for cite observation (TASK-02)`
